heading: Coordination with certain other provisions of law
label: 2306 Coordination with certain other provisions of law
text: No provision of this chapter, or action taken under this chapter, shall be construed to impair the authorities and responsibilities set forth in section 102 of the National Security Act of 1947 (; ), the Central Intelligence Agency Act of 1949 (; and following), the Act entitled “An Act to provide certain administrative authorities for the National Security Agency, and for other purposes”, approved (; note), and the Act entitled “An Act to amend the Internal Security Act of 1950”, approved (; ).61 Stat. 49550 U.S.C. 403163 Stat. 20850 U.S.C. 403a173 Stat. 6350 U.S.C. 402178 Stat. 16850 U.S.C. 831–835See References in Text note below.1May 29, 1959March 26, 1964
notes: References in TextThe National Security Act of 1947, referred to in text, is , , which was formerly classified principally to chapter 15 (§ 401 et seq.) of Title 50, War and National Defense, prior to editorial reclassification in chapter 44 (§ 3001 et seq.) of Title 50. Section 102 of the Act was repealed by , , , another section 102 was repealed by , §§ 1011(a), 1097(a), , , 3698, and subsequently another section 102, as added by , §1011(a), , , was classified to prior to editorial reclassification to .
